SEARCH
Package org.opentcs.components.kernel.routing

Interface EdgeEvaluator

public interface EdgeEvaluator
Computes the weight of edges in the routing graph.

Any routing graph computation is performed with a RoutingContext. Before the very first computation, an edge evaluator is provided with the current routing context via onRoutingContextUpdated(RoutingContext). Subsequent routing context updates always precede a routing graph computation, but do not necessarily imply that the routing context has actually changed.

Implementations that require information from the routing context in computeWeight(Edge, Vehicle) should process/prepare them in onGraphComputationStart(Vehicle).

For every vehicle, the following methods are always executed in the following order:

  1. onGraphComputationStart(Vehicle)
  2. computeWeight(Edge, Vehicle)
  3. onGraphComputationEnd(Vehicle)

Support for parallelization

Implementations that want to support parallelization (indicated by isParallelGraphComputationSupported()) have to be aware that only the following methods may be called in parallel (and only for different vehicles):

  • onGraphComputationStart(Vehicle)
  • computeWeight(Edge, Vehicle)
  • onGraphComputationEnd(Vehicle)

Therefore, implementations must take into account that these methods may be executed in parallel for different vehicles. Implementations must also take into account that onRoutingContextUpdated(RoutingContext), which is not subject to parallelization, and the other parallelized methods may be called from different threads.

Furthermore, implementations that want to support parallelization should only use information contained in the routing context and refrain from querying the kernel for additional information during computation, as this will most likely result in a deadlock situation.

Even if an edge evaluator indicates support for parallelization, this does not necessarily mean that parallelization is actually utilized.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    double
    computeWeight(Edge edge, Vehicle vehicle)
    Computes the weight of an edge in the routing graph.
    default boolean
    isParallelGraphComputationSupported()
    Indicates whether parallel graph computation is supported.
    void
    onGraphComputationEnd(Vehicle vehicle)
    Called when/after a computation of a routing graph is done.
    void
    onGraphComputationStart(Vehicle vehicle)
    Called when/before computation of a routing graph starts.
    default void
    onRoutingContextUpdated(RoutingContext context)
    Called when the routing context has been updated.

  • Method Details

    • isParallelGraphComputationSupported

      @ScheduledApiChange(when="8.0", details="Default implementation will be removed.") default boolean isParallelGraphComputationSupported()
      Indicates whether parallel graph computation is supported.
      Returns:
      true, if parallel graph computation is supported, or false, if only sequential graph computation is supported.

    • onRoutingContextUpdated

      @ScheduledApiChange(when="8.0", details="Default implementation will be removed.") default void onRoutingContextUpdated(@Nonnull RoutingContext context)
      Called when the routing context has been updated.

      Since this method is not subject to parallelization, calls to it should return quickly.

      Parameters:
      context - The routing context.

    • onGraphComputationStart

      void onGraphComputationStart(@Nonnull Vehicle vehicle)
      Called when/before computation of a routing graph starts.
      Parameters:
      vehicle - The vehicle for which the routing graph is computed.

    • onGraphComputationEnd

      void onGraphComputationEnd(@Nonnull Vehicle vehicle)
      Called when/after a computation of a routing graph is done.
      Parameters:
      vehicle - The vehicle for which the routing graph is computed.

    • computeWeight

      double computeWeight(@Nonnull Edge edge, @Nonnull Vehicle vehicle)
      Computes the weight of an edge in the routing graph.
      Parameters:
      edge - The edge.
      vehicle - The vehicle for which to compute the edge's weight.
      Returns:
      The computed weight of the given edge. A value of Double.POSITIVE_INFINITY indicates that the edge is to be excluded from routing. Note that negative weights might not be handled well by the respective routing algorithm used.
